ops.title: Many fleet repos render CLAUDE.md from CLAUDE.src.md via cc-guides; rendered-only edits self-revert on push — always stamp src + rendered together
ops.body:
As of 2026-07-08, ~18 of the ~31 Models-table fleet repos (captain-hook, cc-context, cc-factory, cc-interact, cc-merge, cc-notes, cc-pane, cc-present, cc-review, cc-runtime, cc-squash, cc-steer, cc-transcript, cookiesync, dailies, docker-dsl, spawnllm, synckit) migrated to **cc-guides-generated CLAUDE.md**: the source of truth is `CLAUDE.src.md`, and `.github/workflows/guides.yml` re-renders CLAUDE.md on push (cc-guides 0.1.7 at migration time). The rendered file carries a GENERATED banner naming the source.

**Why:** an edit to the rendered CLAUDE.md alone passes locally and pushes fine, then CI re-renders from the stale `CLAUDE.src.md` and lands a `chore: re-render guides` commit that silently reverts the edit on main@origin. A 31-repo fan-out re-stamp "succeeded" 29/31 by its own report, but only 14 survived on the remotes — the generated repos all self-reverted within minutes.

**How to apply:** any fleet sweep touching CLAUDE.md must check for `CLAUDE.src.md` (or the GENERATED banner) first and edit **both files in one commit** (keeps the freshness check green, no re-render churn). Verify against `main@origin` *after* the guides CI run settles, not at push time — a passing push is not a landed edit. Related: [[model-routing-convention]], [[shared-working-copy-concurrency]].
